title: Fix sorting targetId format mismatch in parseStepMutations
description: parseStepMutations at AlgorithmModule.tsx:82 uses regex /element-(\d+)/ but all sorting engines (bubble-sort.ts:60, quick-sort.ts, etc.) emit targetId as plain String(j) like '0','5'. Regex never matches, so all sorting bars stay gray (default state) throughout animation. Fix: change regex to /(?:element-)?(\d+)/ or update all sorting engines to use 'element-{index}' format.
acceptanceCriteria: Running Bubble Sort on [5,3,8,1] shows blue bars during comparison steps
Running Quick Sort shows purple pivot bar during partitioning
Sorted bars turn green at completion
All 6 runnable sorting algorithms display correct color states

title: Wire 10 missing sorting algorithms to SORTING_RUNNERS
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx:125-132 SORTING_RUNNERS only maps 6 of 16 sorting algorithms. Shell, Counting, Radix, Bucket, Tim, Cocktail-Shaker, Comb, Pancake, Bogo, Radix-MSD are all exported and implemented but not in the runner map. Clicking Run silently returns at line 567. Add all 10 to SORTING_RUNNERS.
acceptanceCriteria: All 16 sorting algorithms in SORTING_ALGORITHMS have matching entries in SORTING_RUNNERS
Selecting Shell Sort and clicking Run produces animation steps
Selecting Tim Sort and clicking Run produces animation steps
No algorithm in the Sorting category silently fails on Run

title: Wire 4 missing tree algorithms (Red-Black, B-Tree, Huffman, Trie) in handleRun
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx handleRun tree section (lines 392-449) only handles bst-operations, avl-tree, tree-traversals, heap-operations. Red-Black (red-black-tree), B-Tree (b-tree), Huffman (huffman-tree), Trie (trie-operations) have engine code but no handleRun cases. Engines: rbInsert, bTreeInsert, huffmanBuild, trieInsertAlgo are exported from barrel.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting Red-Black Tree and clicking Run produces visualization
Selecting B-Tree and clicking Run produces visualization
Selecting Huffman Tree and clicking Run produces visualization
Selecting Trie Operations and clicking Run produces visualization

title: Wire 6 missing DP algorithms (LIS, Matrix-Chain, Rod-Cutting, Subset-Sum, Palindrome, Catalan) in handleRun
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx handleRun DP section (lines 461-510) only handles fibonacci-dp, lcs, edit-distance, knapsack, coin-change. Six DP algorithms have engine code but no handleRun cases: lis, matrix-chain, rod-cutting, subset-sum, longest-palindrome, catalan. All are exported from dp/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting LIS and clicking Run produces DP table visualization
Selecting Matrix Chain Multiplication and clicking Run produces DP table
Selecting Rod Cutting and clicking Run produces visualization
All 11 DP algorithms produce results when Run is clicked

title: Wire 2 missing string algorithms (Boyer-Moore, Z-Algorithm) and 2 backtracking (Knight's Tour, Subset-Generation) in handleRun
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx handleRun string section (lines 512-527) only handles kmp, rabin-karp. Boyer-Moore and Z-Algorithm have engines (boyerMooreSearch, zAlgorithmSearch) but no cases. Backtracking section (lines 528-536) only handles n-queens, sudoku. Knight's Tour (solveKnightsTour) and Subset Generation (generateSubsets) have engines but no cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Boyer-Moore search runs and shows pattern matching visualization
Z-Algorithm search runs and shows visualization
Knight's Tour runs and shows grid visualization
Subset Generation runs and shows results

title: Add onComplete callback to PlaybackController to fix stuck play state
description: playback-controller.ts:128-129 sets this.playing=false when reaching end but fires no callback. AlgorithmPanel.tsx playing state stays true, showing Pause icon. User clicks Play again but ctrl.play() returns immediately at line 33 because currentIndex >= steps.length. Fix: add onComplete callback to constructor, fire it when playback ends. In AlgorithmPanel, set playing=false in the callback.
acceptanceCriteria: After playback reaches the last step, Play button shows Play icon (not Pause)
Clicking Play after natural completion restarts from beginning or does nothing gracefully
Comparison controller also properly reports completion

title: Show error message when clicking Run on unimplemented algorithm
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx:567 silently returns when sortingRunner is undefined. Same for tree:449, dp:510, string:526, backtracking:535. Add visual feedback: show inline error 'This algorithm visualization is coming soon' or disable Run button with tooltip when no runner exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Run on an unwired algorithm shows visible error text near the Run button
Error disappears when switching to a working algorithm
No silent failures — every Run click produces either visualization or error message

title: Fix comparison mode to track separate array state for comparison algorithm
description: AlgorithmModule.tsx:830 passes values={values} (primary array) to comparison ArrayVisualizer. Both sides show the same array. The comparison algorithm sorts its own copy but the sorted state is never tracked/displayed. Fix: maintain comparisonArray in state, update it from comparison controller steps, pass to the comparison visualizer.
acceptanceCriteria: In comparison mode, left side shows primary algorithm's array state
Right side shows comparison algorithm's different array state
Both sides animate independently based on their respective steps

title: Add ErrorBoundary around each visualizer in AlgorithmCanvas
description: AlgorithmModule.tsx:623-938 AlgorithmCanvas renders visualizers without error boundaries. A throw in layoutTree (null tree), graph layout (empty nodes), or any visualizer crashes the entire module. Wrap each visualizer in React ErrorBoundary with 'Something went wrong — try a different algorithm' fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: If TreeVisualizer throws, canvas shows error fallback instead of blank screen
User can select a different algorithm without refreshing
ErrorBoundary resets when algorithm selection changes

title: Add reduced motion support to all 7 visualizers
description: All visualizers (ArrayVisualizer.tsx, GraphVisualizer.tsx, TreeVisualizer.tsx, DPVisualizer.tsx, StringMatchVisualizer.tsx, GridVisualizer.tsx, GeometryVisualizer.tsx) use motion/react animations with no prefers-reduced-motion support. Either configure MotionConfig with reducedMotion='user' at a provider level, or add useReducedMotion hook and skip animations when enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: With prefers-reduced-motion: reduce enabled, animations are instant or disabled
State changes still visible (colors change, but no spring/transition)
Playback still functions correctly with reduced motion

title: Add ARIA roles, labels, and live regions to algorithm panel and visualizers
description: Zero ARIA attributes across entire module. Buttons at AlgorithmPanel.tsx:1230-1260 have title but no aria-label. Visualizers have no role. Step changes not announced. Add: role='region' to visualizers, aria-label to all buttons, aria-live='polite' region for step descriptions, role='status' for step counter.
acceptanceCriteria: All playback buttons have aria-label describing their action
Step description changes are announced to screen readers via aria-live
Visualizer containers have role='img' with aria-label describing current state
Algorithm dropdown has accessible label

title: Extract hardcoded hex colors to shared design token constants
description: 80+ hardcoded hex colors across 7 visualizers: ArrayVisualizer.tsx:12-18, GraphVisualizer.tsx:14-30, TreeVisualizer.tsx:15-24, DPVisualizer.tsx:16-38, StringMatchVisualizer.tsx:17-33, GridVisualizer.tsx:21-48, GeometryVisualizer.tsx:14-22. Create shared ALGO_STATE_COLORS constant (or CSS custom properties) and import in all visualizers. Existing src/lib/visualization/colors.ts has ALGO_STATE_COLORS but it's not used by active overlays.
acceptanceCriteria: All visualizer state colors come from a single shared source
Changing a color in one place updates all visualizers
No hardcoded hex in any visualizer component file for state colors
Dark/light theme switching works if tokens use CSS custom properties

title: Add color-independent state indicators (patterns/shapes) to visualizers
description: All visualizers use color as sole differentiator for states. Colorblind users cannot distinguish comparing (blue) from swapping (red) from sorted (green). Add secondary indicators: hatching patterns for comparing, dot patterns for swapping, solid fills for sorted, border dashes for pivot. Start with ArrayVisualizer as proof-of-concept.
acceptanceCriteria: ArrayVisualizer bars have distinct visual patterns in addition to colors
A monochrome screenshot still shows which bars are comparing vs swapping vs sorted
Legend includes both color and pattern

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for playback controls
description: No keyboard event handlers in AlgorithmPanel.tsx or AlgorithmModule.tsx. Add: Space=play/pause, Left=step-backward, Right=step-forward, Home=stop, 1-5=speed presets. Use global keydown listener with proper focus management so shortcuts work when any module element is focused.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Space toggles play/pause when algorithm panel has focus
Left/Right arrows step backward/forward
Number keys 1-5 change speed
Shortcuts don't fire when typing in input fields

title: De-duplicate algorithm config: single source of truth per engine
description: Each sorting algorithm has its config defined twice: once in the engine file (e.g., bubble-sort.ts:12-34 CONFIG) and once in sorting/index.ts:26-47 SORTING_ALGORITHMS array. Same for all categories. These can drift. Fix: have SORTING_ALGORITHMS import config from each engine, e.g., import { CONFIG as BUBBLE_SORT_CONFIG } from './bubble-sort', or have engines export config and barrel re-export.
acceptanceCriteria: Each algorithm config is defined exactly once
SORTING_ALGORITHMS/GRAPH_ALGORITHMS/etc. arrays reference the engine configs
No duplicate description/complexity/pseudocode definitions

title: Add step description overlay on canvas (reduce split-attention)
description: Step description is in the properties panel (AlgorithmModule.tsx:484-494) while visualization is in the canvas (AlgorithmModule.tsx:842+). User must look back and forth — classic split-attention effect violating Mayer's Spatial Contiguity principle. Add a semi-transparent overlay at the top or bottom of the canvas showing the current step description and pseudocode line.
acceptanceCriteria: Current step description appears overlaid on the canvas area
Overlay doesn't obstruct the visualization significantly
Overlay updates in sync with step changes
User can still access full step detail in properties panel

title: Replace native select with searchable algorithm combobox
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx:685-721 uses native <select> with <optgroup> for 62 algorithms. No search, no filtering. As the catalog grows, this becomes unusable. Replace with a searchable combobox (headless UI or custom) that supports typing to filter, keyboard navigation, and category grouping.
acceptanceCriteria: User can type to filter algorithms by name
Categories are still visible as group headers
Keyboard navigation (up/down/enter) works
Selected algorithm is clearly indicated

title: Add WHY explanations to sorting step descriptions
description: Step descriptions (e.g., bubble-sort.ts:77 'Compare arr[0]=5 with arr[1]=3') say WHAT but not WHY. Educational tools must explain reasoning. Add pedagogical context: 'Compare adjacent elements — if left > right, they're out of order and need swapping' on first occurrence, then shorter on subsequent. Apply to all 6 runnable sorting engines initially.
acceptanceCriteria: First comparison step includes WHY explanation
First swap step explains why the swap is needed
Sorted-marking step explains what it means to be in final position
Descriptions are readable by a beginner with no prior sorting knowledge

title: Move engine execution to existing Web Worker
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx:389-605 runs all engines synchronously on the main thread. Large inputs (array of 100+ elements, N-Queens with N=12) can freeze the UI. src/lib/workers/algorithm-worker.ts already exists but is unused. Move engine calls to the worker, show a loading spinner during computation, and handle results via message passing.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine computation happens in Web Worker, not main thread
UI remains responsive during computation
Loading indicator shown while worker is computing
Error from worker is caught and displayed to user

title: Auto-play algorithm after clicking Run
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx:602 sets playing=false after creating PlaybackController. User must click Run, then separately click Play — 2 actions for 1 intent. Change behavior: after Run creates the controller, automatically start playback (ctrl.play() + setPlaying(true)). Add a user preference toggle if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Run immediately starts animation playback
Play button shows Pause icon after Run
User can still Pause to stop auto-play
Step counter advances immediately after Run

title: Memoize allAlgorithms array to prevent recreation every render
description: AlgorithmModule.tsx:299, 522, 660 and AlgorithmPanel.tsx:313 all create [...SORTING_ALGORITHMS, ...GRAPH_ALGORITHMS, ...TREE_ALGORITHMS, ...DP_ALGORITHMS, ...STRING_ALGORITHMS, ...BACKTRACKING_ALGORITHMS, ...GEOMETRY_ALGORITHMS] on every render. Extract to module-level constant (arrays are static) or useMemo.
acceptanceCriteria: allAlgorithms is defined once at module scope or memoized
No spread operations for static arrays inside render functions
All 4 usage sites reference the shared constant

title: Increase touch target sizes for mobile compliance
description: Playback buttons at AlgorithmPanel.tsx:1230-1260 are h-7 w-7 (28x28px). Play button is h-8 w-8 (32x32px). Speed buttons are ~30px. All below WCAG 44x44px minimum. Increase all interactive targets to minimum 44x44px using padding or sizing.
acceptanceCriteria: All playback buttons have minimum 44x44px touch target
Speed selector buttons have minimum 44x44px touch target
Preset buttons have minimum 44x44px touch target
Visual appearance remains clean (use padding not just button size if needed)

title: Add parseStepMutations and PlaybackController unit tests
description: parseStepMutations (AlgorithmModule.tsx:71-106) has zero tests — the targetId format mismatch bug would have been caught. PlaybackController (playback-controller.ts) has zero tests — the stuck-play-state bug would have been caught. Write unit tests for both: various targetId formats, edge cases (empty mutations, invalid indices), and controller lifecycle (play, pause, stop, completion, speed change).
acceptanceCriteria: parseStepMutations test covers: element-N format, plain-N format, mixed formats, empty mutations, out-of-bounds index
PlaybackController test covers: constructor, play/pause/stop, stepForward/stepBackward, completion callback, speed change, destroy
Tests run and pass in CI

title: Add integration test: all algorithm IDs have matching runners
description: Write a test that iterates all algorithm config arrays (SORTING_ALGORITHMS, GRAPH_ALGORITHMS, etc.), and for each algorithm ID, verifies a runner exists in the corresponding runner map or handleRun code path. This prevents future regressions where a new algorithm is added to config but not wired up.
acceptanceCriteria: Test iterates all 62 algorithm IDs across 7 categories
Test fails if any algorithm ID lacks a runner mapping
Test runs in CI and would have caught the 24 missing runners

title: Delete dead duplicate visualizer files under visualization/algorithms/
description: src/components/visualization/algorithms/ contains SortingVisualizer.tsx, GraphVisualizer.tsx, StringMatchVisualizer.tsx, DPTableVisualizer.tsx. These are NOT imported by AlgorithmModule or any active component. They use a different color system (ALGO_STATE_COLORS from lib/visualization/colors) than the active overlays. Delete or consolidate.
acceptanceCriteria: Duplicate visualizer files are removed
No broken imports after deletion
Active visualizers under canvas/overlays/ remain functional

title: Split AlgorithmModule.tsx (1201 lines) and AlgorithmPanel.tsx (1415 lines) into smaller files
description: AlgorithmModule.tsx contains AlgorithmSidebar, AlgorithmProperties, AlgorithmBottomPanel, AlgorithmCanvas, ComplexityComparisonChart, useAlgorithmModule, parseStepMutations, theoreticalComparisons — all in one 1201-line file. AlgorithmPanel.tsx is 1415 lines with handleRun spanning 200+ lines. Extract: AlgorithmCanvas into own file, AlgorithmProperties into own file, BottomPanel into own file, handleRun into per-category handler files.
acceptanceCriteria: No file exceeds 500 lines
Each component is in its own file
handleRun is split into handleRunSorting, handleRunGraph, etc.
All functionality preserved

title: Reset DP/String/Backtracking input state when switching algorithms
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx:688-708 resets some state on algorithm switch but not category-specific inputs. dpString1, dpString2, dpNumber, dpCoins, dpItems, searchText, searchPattern, treeValueInput persist when switching between algorithms in the same category. User sees stale input from previous algorithm.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching from LCS to Edit Distance clears dpString1/dpString2
Switching from Knapsack to Coin Change clears dpItems
Switching from KMP to Rabin-Karp clears searchText/searchPattern
Only inputs relevant to the selected algorithm are shown

title: Add max-length guard on array text input to prevent memory exhaustion
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx:1140 textarea has no maxLength. User could paste 100,000 comma-separated numbers, causing the engine to run synchronously and freeze/crash. Add maxLength={500} (roughly 100 elements) and show character count or element count.
acceptanceCriteria: Array input textarea has maxLength attribute
User sees feedback when approaching the limit
Pasting very large data is truncated gracefully

title: Standardize naming: selectedId vs selectedAlgoId
description: AlgorithmPanel.tsx:273 uses selectedId while AlgorithmModule.tsx:948 uses selectedAlgoId for the same concept. Standardize to selectedAlgoId across both files for consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Both files use the same variable name for the selected algorithm ID
No functional regressions

title: Add difficulty labels and learning path ordering to algorithm catalog
description: All 62 algorithms are presented equally with no difficulty indicators. Add difficulty field (beginner/intermediate/advanced/expert) to AlgorithmConfig type. Add recommended-order field. Show difficulty badge next to algorithm name in dropdown. Consider adding 'Suggested for beginners' section at top of dropdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Each algorithm has a difficulty level visible in the dropdown
Difficulty badges are color-coded (green/amber/red/purple)
A 'Start Here' or 'Beginner' section appears at the top of the dropdown
Difficulty data defined in algorithm configs

title: Add auto-generated sample data on first load for instant visualization
description: Currently the canvas shows an empty state on load (AlgorithmModule.tsx:853-933). User sees 'Select a sorting algorithm...' text. Instead, auto-generate a small sample array (e.g., 10 elements) on first render and show it in the visualizer immediately. This gives an instant 'wow' moment and shows the user what the tool does before any interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: On first load, ArrayVisualizer shows a pre-generated array of 10 colored bars
Array input field is populated with the sample data
User can immediately click Run to see Bubble Sort animate
Time from page load to seeing bars is <1 second

title: Add worst-case input finder with scoring for sorting algorithms
description: Validated by 3-agent review + dpvis SIGCSE 2025 paper showing self-testing improves DP learning. Add 'Worst Case Challenge' mode: user crafts an input array aiming to maximize comparisons/swaps. Tool runs the selected sorting algorithm, displays step count, compares against known theoretical worst-case (already in AlgorithmConfig.timeComplexity.worst). Score = actual_steps / theoretical_worst_steps * 100. Leaderboard for best (worst) inputs per algorithm. Start with sorting only.
acceptanceCriteria: Worst Case Challenge button visible in sidebar when a sorting algorithm is selected
User can type a custom array and see comparison/swap count after run
Score displayed as percentage of theoretical worst case achieved
Known worst-case hint shown after 3 failed attempts (e.g., 'Try a reverse-sorted array')
Works for all 16 sorting algorithms

title: Add 'Predict the Next Step' interactive mode
description: Instead of passively watching, user clicks 'Predict Mode'. Animation pauses before each step. User is asked 'What happens next?' with multiple choice options (e.g., 'Compare 5 and 3', 'Swap 5 and 3', 'Mark as sorted'). Correct answer advances; wrong answer shows explanation. This is active learning (Mayer's Generative Activity principle). Build as a wrapper around PlaybackController that pauses before each step.
acceptanceCriteria: Predict Mode toggle visible in playback section
In Predict Mode, playback pauses before each step
3 multiple-choice options shown based on current state
Correct answer advances with positive feedback
Wrong answer shows explanation of what actually happens and why
Score tracked during session (N/M correct)

title: Add one-click export of algorithm animation as GIF/video
description: Use MediaRecorder API or canvas-based frame capture to export the current algorithm animation as a shareable GIF or MP4. Add 'Export' button in playback controls. Record the canvas area during playback, add algorithm name as watermark, and offer download. This makes the tool viral — students share animations in study groups, professors use in slides.
acceptanceCriteria: Export button visible after running an algorithm
Clicking Export records a full playback and downloads as MP4/GIF
Output includes algorithm name and step count
File size is reasonable (<5MB for typical animation)

title: Add mobile-responsive layout with touch gesture controls
description: Every competitor comparison article (daily.dev, codewave.com 2026) cites 'no mobile optimization' as a universal gap. AlgorithmPanel sidebar is fixed-width, canvas collapses on narrow screens, playback buttons are 28x28px (below 44px min). Add: collapsible sidebar as bottom sheet on mobile, swipe left/right for step forward/backward, pinch-zoom on visualizers, bottom-anchored playback controls, breakpoints at 375px and 768px.
acceptanceCriteria: At viewport width <=768px, sidebar collapses to a bottom sheet with algorithm selector
Swipe left/right on the canvas steps forward/backward through algorithm steps
All playback buttons meet 44x44px minimum touch target on mobile
Visualization is readable and usable at 375px width (iPhone SE)
No horizontal scrolling at any supported viewport width

title: Add code-alongside-visualization panel showing algorithm source code
description: Top user request per codewave.com 2026 analysis: 'IDE integration / real-time code feedback.' Algorithm Visualizer (47k GitHub stars) wins over VisuAlgo specifically because it shows code alongside visualization. Add a toggleable code panel that displays the actual TypeScript engine source for the selected algorithm (e.g., bubble-sort.ts), with the current line highlighted in sync with pseudocodeLine from AnimationStep. Reuse existing pseudocode highlighting logic from AlgorithmModule.tsx:460.
acceptanceCriteria: Code panel toggleable via button in properties panel header
Shows the actual engine source code for the selected algorithm
Current execution line is highlighted in sync with animation step's pseudocodeLine
Syntax highlighting for TypeScript code
Panel is scrollable and doesn't obstruct the visualization

title: Add scoring system with stars and progress tracking per algorithm
description: DSA Visualizer (visualizedsa.com) has 'Test Your Knowledge' with +1 per correct answer and star ratings (max 5). VisuAlgo has auto-grading. Architex has neither. Add: progress state per algorithm (not-started/in-progress/completed), completion = watched all steps to end, stars earned from Predict Mode (ALG-174) score. Show progress bar in sidebar: 'Sorting: 3/16 completed ★★☆'. Persist in localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Each algorithm shows completion status icon (empty/half/full circle) in the dropdown
Watching an algorithm to the final step marks it as 'completed'
Progress bar shows 'X/Y completed' per category in sidebar
Stars (1-3) awarded based on Predict Mode score if available
Progress persists across page refreshes via localStorage
Reset button to clear all progress

title: Add recursive call stack visualizer for recursive algorithms
description: Stack Overflow research confirms recursion and DP are the most-confused topics. No current visualizer shows the recursive call tree alongside the algorithm animation. For recursive algorithms (Quick Sort, Merge Sort, tree traversals, N-Queens), add a collapsible call stack panel showing: current call depth, parameters, return values, which call is active. Renders as a nested tree view that grows/shrinks with each recursive call/return. Inspired by VS Code debugger call stack panel.
acceptanceCriteria: When running Merge Sort, a call stack panel shows the current recursive call tree
Active call is highlighted, completed calls are grayed out
Each call shows its parameters (e.g., mergeSort(arr, 0, 4))
Call stack updates in sync with animation steps
Panel is collapsible/toggleable to not overwhelm beginners

title: Add dpvis-style self-testing to DP visualizer (must-answer-before-advance)
description: dpvis (SIGCSE 2025, 19/24 students found useful) uses a self-testing feature where students must answer 'What cells are written to next?' and 'What is the value of cell (x,y)?' before the animation advances. 75% of initially uncomfortable students reported positive impact. Implement similar for Architex DPVisualizer: in test mode, pause before each step, ask which cell is computed next and what its value will be. Requires answer before advancing. Track correct/total score.
acceptanceCriteria: Test Mode toggle visible when viewing any DP algorithm
In Test Mode, animation pauses before each cell computation
User is asked 'Which cell will be computed next?' with clickable cells
After selecting the cell, user is asked 'What will its value be?' with numeric input
Correct answer advances with green feedback, wrong answer shows explanation
Score displayed as fraction (e.g., 15/23 correct)

title: Add prerequisite links between algorithms ('Learn BFS before Dijkstra')
description: D19 finding: no prerequisites stated anywhere. Add prerequisite field to AlgorithmConfig. When user selects an advanced algorithm, show a gentle notice: 'This builds on BFS — have you seen it yet?' with link to the prerequisite. If prerequisite is not completed (per ALG-178 progress tracking), show recommendation but do NOT gate access. VisuAlgo does this with structured e-lectures.
acceptanceCriteria: AlgorithmConfig type has optional prerequisites: string[] field
At least 15 algorithms have prerequisites defined (e.g., Dijkstra needs BFS, AVL needs BST)
When selecting an algorithm with unmet prerequisites, an inline notice appears
Notice links to the prerequisite algorithm (clicking navigates to it)
Notice is dismissible and does not block usage
